heading: Application for grant; approval of State plan
label: 300x–32 Application for grant; approval of State plan

children.text: the application is received by the Secretary not later than October 1 of the fiscal year for which the State is seeking funds;
the application contains each funding agreement that is described in this subpart or subpart III for such a grant (other than any such agreement that is not applicable to the State);
the agreements are made through certification from the chief executive officer of the State;
with respect to such agreements, the application provides assurances of compliance satisfactory to the Secretary;
the application contains the report required in ;section 300x–52(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the application contains a plan in accordance with subsection (b) and the plan is approved by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the State provides assurances satisfactory to the Secretary that the State complied with the provisions of the plan under subparagraph (A) that was approved by the Secretary for the most recent fiscal year for which the State received a grant under ; andsection 300x–21 of this title

the application (including the plan under paragraph (6)) is otherwise in such form, is made in such manner, and contains such agreements, assurances, and information as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this subpart.

children.label: i
children.text: identifies the single State agency responsible for the administration of the program, including any third party who administers substance use disorder services and is responsible for complying with the requirements of the grant;

children.label: ii
children.text: provides information on the need for substance use disorder prevention and treatment services in the State, including estimates on the number of individuals who need treatment, who are pregnant women, women with dependent children, individuals with a co-occurring mental health and substance use disorder, persons who inject drugs, and persons who are experiencing homelessness;

children.label: iii
children.text: provides aggregate information on the number of individuals in treatment within the State, including the number of such individuals who are pregnant women, women with dependent children, individuals with a co-occurring mental health and substance use disorder, persons who inject drugs, and persons who are experiencing homelessness;

children.label: iv
children.text: provides a description of the system that is available to provide services by modality, including the provision of recovery support services;

children.label: v
children.text: provides a description of the State’s comprehensive statewide prevention efforts, including the number of individuals being served in the system, target populations, and priority needs, and provides a description of the amount of funds from the prevention set-aside expended on primary prevention;

children.label: I
children.text: the State’s comprehensive statewide recovery support services activities, including the number of individuals being served, target populations, workforce capacity (consistent with clause (viii)), and priority needs; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of funds received under this subpart expended on recovery support services, disaggregated by the amount expended for type of service activity;

children.label: vii
children.text: provides a description of the financial resources available;

children.label: viii
children.text: describes the existing substance use disorders workforce, including with respect to prevention, treatment, and recovery, and workforce trained in treating co-occurring substance use and mental disorders;

children.label: ix
children.text: includes a description of how the State promotes evidence-based practices; and

children.label: x
children.text: describes how the State integrates substance use disorder services and primary health care, which in the case of those individuals with co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders may include providing both mental health and substance use disorder services in primary care settings or providing primary and specialty care services in community-based mental health and substance use disorder service settings.

children.label: B
children.text: The establishment of goals and objectives for the period of the plan, including targets and milestones that are intended to be met, and the activities that will be undertaken to achieve those targets.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of how the State will comply with each funding agreement for a grant under that is applicable to the State, including a description of the manner in which the State intends to expend grant funds.section 300x–21 of this title

children.heading: Authority of Secretary
children.label: A Authority of Secretary
children.text: As a condition; of making a grant under to a State for a fiscal year, the Secretary may require that the State modify any provision of the plan submitted by the State under subsection (a)(6) (including provisions on priorities in carrying out authorized activities). If the Secretary approves the plan and makes the grant to the State for the fiscal year, the Secretary may not during such year require the State to modify the plan.1section 300x–21 of this titleSo in original. The semicolon probably should not appear.1

children.heading: State request for modification
children.label: B State request for modification
children.text: If the State determines that a modification to such plan is necessary, the State may request the Secretary to approve the modification. Any such modification shall be in accordance with paragraph (1) and .section 300x–51 of this title

children.heading: Authority of Center for Substance Abuse Prevention
children.label: 3 Authority of Center for Substance Abuse Prevention
children.text: With respect to plans submitted by the States under subsection (a)(6), including any modification under paragraph (2), the Secretary, acting through the Director of the Center for Substance Abuse Prevention, shall review and approve or disapprove the provisions of the plans that relate to prevention activities.

children.heading: Waivers regarding certain territories
children.label: c Waivers regarding certain territories
children.text: In the case of any territory of the United States except Puerto Rico, the Secretary may waive such provisions of this subpart and subpart III as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, other than the provisions of .section 300x–31 of this title

children.heading: Issuance of regulations; precondition to making grants
children.label: d Issuance of regulations; precondition to making grants

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 1 Regulations
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary, acting as appropriate through the Director of the Center for Treatment Improvement or the Director of the Center for Substance Abuse Prevention, shall by regulation establish standards specifying the circumstances in which the Secretary will consider an application for a grant under to be in accordance with this section.August 25, 1992section 300x–21 of this title

children.heading: Issuance as precondition to making grants
children.label: 2 Issuance as precondition to making grants
children.text: The Secretary may not make payments under any grant under for fiscal year 1993 on or after , unless the Secretary has issued standards under paragraph (1).section 300x–21 of this titleJanuary 1, 1993

children.heading: Waiver authority for certain requirements
children.label: e Waiver authority for certain requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Upon the request of a State, the Secretary may waive the requirements of all or part of the sections described in paragraph (2) using objective criteria established by the Secretary by regulation after consultation with the States and other interested parties including consumers and providers.

children.heading: Sections
children.label: 2 Sections
children.text: The sections described in paragraph (1) are sections 300x–22(b), 300x–23, 300x–24 and 300x–28 of this title.

children.heading: Date certain for acting upon request
children.label: 3 Date certain for acting upon request
children.text: The Secretary shall approve or deny a request for a waiver under paragraph (1) and inform the State of that decision not later than 120 days after the date on which the request and all the information needed to support the request are submitted.

children.heading: Annual reporting requirement
children.label: 4 Annual reporting requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall annually report to the general public on the States that receive a waiver under this subsection.
